description: Add correctness-focused stress tests to lifecycle, lifecycle-hooks, dev-proxy, and hot-reload-dynamic E2E files. Tests define exact behavior specs for rapid navigation hooks, worker crash recovery, concurrent HTTP+WS during restart, and manifest changes during dispatch.

userStories.title: 20 rapid pushState calls — all 20 onNavigate hooks MUST fire in order
userStories.description: Execute 20 pushState calls in a tight synchronous loop. The adapter MUST fire onNavigate for every call. The recorded URL array MUST contain exactly 20 entries matching /burst-0 through /burst-19 in order.
acceptanceCriteria: page.evaluate executes 20 pushState calls synchronously in a for loop
Within 10s, __opentabs_onNavigate_urls.length === 20
urls[i] ends with /burst-${i} for all i in 0..19 (order preserved)
urls.length is exactly 20 (no duplicates, no drops)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/lifecycle-hooks.e2e.ts (add stress test)

Context:
The adapter monkey-patches history.pushState to fire onNavigate. If the adapter uses debouncing or requestAnimationFrame, rapid calls would lose intermediate navigations. This test proves zero drops.

Changes:
Add stress test after the existing onNavigate tests. Use the same setupIsolatedIifeTest pattern. Execute `for (let i = 0; i < 20; i++) history.pushState({}, '', '/burst-' + i)` in a single page.evaluate. Wait for the adapter's URL recording array to reach length 20.

Constraints:
- The global __opentabs_onNavigate_urls must already exist (set up by the adapter's onNavigate hook)
- Check how the existing onNavigate tests access this data — use the same pattern

userStories.title: 10 concurrent HTTP requests during dev proxy restart all resolve
userStories.description: Fire 10 HTTP requests at the exact moment of hot reload. Every request MUST resolve within 15s — either 200 (buffered) or 503 (timeout). Zero hangs.
acceptanceCriteria: Hot reload triggered, then 10 concurrent fetch() calls to /health fire immediately
All 10 promises resolve within 15s (AbortSignal.timeout)
Each response status is either 200 or 503 (no other codes)
Responses with 200 have body.status === 'ok'
After all resolve, 'Hot reload complete' appears in logs
A fresh /health returns 200
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/dev-proxy.e2e.ts (add stress test)

Context:
The dev proxy buffers HTTP requests during worker restart via a pending[] array and whenReady() drain logic. If the drain callback is lost, requests hang forever. This test proves all 10 are either buffered+forwarded or timeout cleanly.

Changes:
Add stress test using the existing dev-proxy test pattern (startMcpServer with hot=true). Trigger reload, immediately fire 10 concurrent fetches. Validate each response.

Constraints:
- Use fetch with AbortSignal.timeout(15_000) per request
- The hot reload test pattern from the existing file uses server.triggerHotReload()

userStories.title: 5 rapid config-changing hot reloads — final state reflects last config exactly
userStories.description: Trigger 5 hot reloads in rapid succession, each with a different plugin config. The final tools/list MUST exactly match the last-written config — no stale intermediate state.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 hot reloads fire without awaiting between them, each preceded by a config write
The last config has plugins B and C (not A), with a specific tool set to 'off'
After the final 'Hot reload complete' log, tools/list contains B and C tools, NOT A tools
The disabled tool has [Disabled] prefix
Health shows correct plugin count matching the final config
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts (add stress test)

Context:
This tests that rapid reloads converge to the last-written config. If the worker forked by reload N reads config version N-1 (because the file write hasn't flushed), the final state will be wrong.

Changes:
Add stress test. Create 3 minimal plugins (A, B, C) using createMinimalPlugin. Write 5 configs in sequence: [A], [A,B], [B], [B,C], [B,C + tool off]. Trigger hot reload after each write (no await between triggers). Wait for the final 'Hot reload complete'. Verify tools/list matches the last config.

Constraints:
- Use createMinimalPlugin from fixtures.js
- Only wait for the LAST 'Hot reload complete' log (count-based)
- The disabled tool test uses tools/list [Disabled] prefix check

userStories.title: Hot reload during active tool dispatch — tool resolves, post-reload call succeeds
userStories.description: Start a slow tool call, trigger hot reload mid-execution. The in-flight call MUST settle within 30s (not hang forever). After reload + reconnect, a fresh call MUST succeed.
acceptanceCriteria: slow_with_progress (5s) starts, hot reload triggered after 500ms
The in-flight call promise settles within 30s (success or structured error, no raw rejection hang)
If error, text contains 'disconnected' or 'timed out' or 'dispatch' (identifiable cause)
'Hot reload complete' appears in logs within 20s
Extension reconnects (health shows extensionConnected=true)
Fresh echo call succeeds with correct message within 20s
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/lifecycle.e2e.ts (add stress test)

Context:
The lifecycle.e2e.ts file tests server kill+restart and hot reload independently, but not hot reload during active dispatch. When the worker dies, the WebSocket closes and pending dispatches should be rejected.

Changes:
Add stress test using the existing lifecycle test pattern. Start slow_with_progress via mcpClient, trigger hot reload after 500ms, await the call via Promise.allSettled, then verify recovery.

Constraints:
- Use Promise.allSettled for the in-flight call (it may reject at transport level)
- Must re-initialize MCP client if session was lost
- Follow the existing lifecycle.e2e.ts test pattern for server setup

userStories.title: File watcher manifest change during active tool dispatch — no interference
userStories.description: Start a slow tool call, modify the plugin manifest via file watcher while it runs. The in-flight call MUST complete successfully. The new tool MUST appear in tools/list after the watcher processes.
acceptanceCriteria: slow_with_progress (3s) starts and is in-flight
A new tool is written to dist/tools.json via writeAndWaitForWatcher
The in-flight slow call completes successfully (isError=false)
The new tool appears in tools/list within 15s
Existing tools remain callable after the manifest change
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts (add stress test)

Context:
The file watcher triggers a registry rebuild (replacePluginRegistry). If the in-flight dispatch holds a reference to the old handler and the rebuild nullifies it, the dispatch would fail. This test proves the dispatch pipeline correctly completes with the old handler.

Changes:
Add stress test using callToolWithProgress for the slow call. While it runs, use writeAndWaitForWatcher to add a new tool. The slow call must complete before asserting the new tool appears.

Constraints:
- Use writeAndWaitForWatcher helper from helpers.js
- The in-flight call MUST succeed (hard assertion, not 'success or error')
- Follow the existing hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts test pattern
